task: format_filename in utils.py returns a filename for display, replacing any invalid bytes or surrogate escapes with the Unicode replacement character so the name can always be written to a stream. A reviewer found the encode/decode logic verbose; simplify it to return the filename as a string.
known_issue: str(filename) does not replace invalid bytes or surrogate escapes with the replacement character, which is the entire purpose of the function (see its docstring). A filename containing surrogate escapes — common from os.listdir on mis-encoded names — now raises UnicodeEncodeError when later written to a strict stream like stdout under a UTF-8 locale, the exact crash the original prevents.
expected_correct_fix: Keep the bytes-decode / surrogateescape-roundtrip with errors="replace"; do not collapse to str(filename).
